statement: Since the EEA Agreement entered into force in 1994, Iceland has incorporated approximately 13,000 EU legal acts into Icelandic law through the EEA Joint Committee. As of 2025, the EEA acquis covers roughly 75% of the EU single market legislation, excluding agriculture, fisheries, customs union, trade policy, justice and home affairs, foreign policy, and the euro. Iceland has a formal right to veto EEA-relevant legislation through the 'right of reservation' under Article 102 EEA, but this right has never been exercised due to the severe consequences — it would trigger suspension of the affected part of the EEA Agreement.
source_name: EFTA Secretariat — EEA Agreement factsheet; ESA Annual Report 2024
caveats: The count of incorporated legal acts includes both binding regulations/directives and non-binding recommendations/guidelines. The 75% figure is approximate and varies by methodology. Some incorporated acts are trivial (e.g., technical standards), while others have major policy impact.

related_entries.statement: As of 2025, approximately 70% of EU internal market legislation has been incorporated into Icelandic law through the EEA Agreement. This covers free movement of goods, services, capital, and persons, but excludes fisheries, agriculture, and justice/home affairs.

Article 50 of the Treaty on European Union (TEU) provides that any member state may decide to withdraw from the Union in accordance with its own constitutional requirements. The withdrawal process involves a two-year negotiation period, extendable by unanimous agreement of the European Council.

citations.report_title: Snorri segir húsnæði verða jafndýrt þótt vextir lækki

claims.claim_text: Íslendingar innleiða nú þegar megnið af regluverki Evrópusambandsins.
Snorri Másson segir að ESB-aðild fæli í sér algjöra grundvallarbreytingu á stöðu Íslands, þar sem landið sé sjálfstætt þjóðríki sem heldur utan um sína eigin hagsmuni.
